name: libmodule-signature-perl
title: libmodule-signature-perl
summary: module to manipulate CPAN SIGNATURE files
description:
module to manipulate CPAN SIGNATURE files
Module::Signature is a Perl module that adds cryptographic authentications to
CPAN distributions, via the special SIGNATURE file. It also includes the
cpansign tool to sign distributions and to verify signatures, and supports
using many different cryptographic hashing algorithms.
.
DEPRECATION NOTICE: Module::Signature has been deprecated because it does not
provide the user with the security assurance that its usage would imply.
.
Module authors should remove the SIGNATURE file when they upload new versions
to PAUSE. Module users should uninstall Module::Signature.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use cpansign because module to manipulate CPAN SIGNATURE files.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use cpansign as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair cpansign with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `cpansign --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install libmodule-signature-perl. module to manipulate CPAN SIGNATURE files.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: module to manipulate CPAN SIGNATURE files.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: module to manipulate CPAN SIGNATURE files.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install libmodule-signature-perl
